channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Tree is damaged problem, 4th June
item.description:
Beech Tree has a disease and looks dead and needs removing
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Blocking visibility on road/junction (Grass/verge) problem, 4th June
item.description:
Clarification Needed – Grass Verge See attached email trace
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: TMC - Defects - Vegetation problem, 4th June
item.description:
28day with Standard TM with fway closure cut back overgrown hedges to boundary approx 60m x 1m x2.5m (Overgrown Hedge)
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: I reported a sink hole last week outside my driveway and this was temporarily fixed on 28th May. A delivery drove it this morning and as you can see from the photo the ground gave way. The hole is a good 30cm wide and deep and poses a safety hazard., 4th June
item.description:
Outside the driveway to number 18

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 3, Jersey Avenue, Ewens Farm, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The entire length of Carlton Street has a number of surface issues, 4th June
item.description:
The entire length of Carlton street has issues. There was a sink hole repaired outside 58 Carlton Street and this is already showing signs of sinking again. Please can you recover the whole of Carlton street to avoid the multiple issues.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 30, Carlton Street, Ewens Farm, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Potholes, 4th June

On the right hand side of inside lane northbound M5 about 1/4 mile before J11 turn off just near the sigh to Glos and Chelt turn off

item.title: Tree falling into subway, 4th June
item.description:
The wheelchair acessible part before going underneath the subway , unsure of how long its been present but serious obstruction for people with disabilities / wheelchairs / children in pushchair
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 5, Miserden Road, Hatherley, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Other damage to pavement i.e. cobbles, raised mound/surface

item.title: Dangerous parking causing accidents; road rage and congestion, 4th June
item.description:
I am writing to raise serious road safety concerns regarding the area around Cleeve School, particularly the junction of Two Hedges Road and Linworth Road in Bishops Cleeve. Cars are parked all along Linworth Road right up until the corner which means it causes unsafe turning and congestion. This is the exact area the causes the issues. The level of parking and congestion during school drop-off and pick-up times has created what I believe is a significant danger to both motorists and pedestrians, especially school children. I have personally witnessed multiple incidents at this location, including a bus colliding with a car, other vehicle collisions, and several road rage incidents caused by traffic backing up around the junction. The situation appears to be worsening and is becoming increasingly hazardous. A particular concern is that vehicles are often forced to manoeuvre around parked cars and congestion, with some drivers mounting or driving along the footway in order to pass. Given the number of children walking to and from school, this presents an obvious and unacceptable safety risk. I would respectfully request that Gloucestershire Highways undertake a road safety assessment of this location and consider whether additional measures are required. These could include parking restrictions, double yellow lines, improved enforcement, or any other traffic management measures deemed appropriate. I believe urgent attention is needed before a more serious incident occurs involving a pedestrian or child. I would be grateful if you could confirm receipt of this email and advise what steps can be taken to investigate these concerns. Yours faithfully, Casey Hill Bishops Cleeve Resident
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Two Hedges Road, Bishop's Cleeve, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: TMC - Defects - Vegetation problem, 4th June
item.description:
Damaged Tree (Limb snapped off and resting on a hedge). Requires Arbs team to cut off branch. This has been made safe with barriers and Footpath Closed signs. TM: Give and Take Outside No 9 (Damaged Tree)
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bridleway is not accessible, 4th June
item.description:
The Bridleway has always been tricky but now there is a sign saying permissive path closed I’m hoping local walkers horse riders will be able to use this Bridleway to link up to the new “green bridge” missing link development
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Greenway Lane, Shurdington, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bus stop sign is missing, 4th June
item.description:
Green Lane bus stop closest to new Kendrick Homes Crickley Fields estate on Shurdington Road, Brockworth

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Shurdington Road, Brockworth, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fallen branch from tree, 4th June
item.description:
A branch has snapped from this tree it was blocking the path. Someone has moved it across to unblock path. But this is dangerous and needs to be removed. It is still attached to the tree.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 5,6, The Cornfields, Bishop's Cleeve, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet
